metadata.title: [REVIEW] Missing data normalization function in API layer

output.summary: Task execution completed with exit code 0
output.implementationNotes:
Task execution completed. Check logs for details.

Final output:
**Findings**
- High (fixed): `src/api/tasks.js` returned `response.data` from `dataService` (already unwrapped), which could yield `undefined` for array/object payloads and drop task/timer data; added response normalization and applied it across task and timer endpoints to stabilize shapes (`src/api/tasks.js:3`, `src/api/tasks.js:102`).
- Medium: `src/__tests__/tasksApi.test.js` mocks `axios`, but `src/api/tasks.js` uses `dataService`, so the tests no longer reflect real behavior and fail with “Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'get/post')” (`src/__tests__/tasksApi.test.js:1`).

Added task/timer response normalization in `src/api/tasks.js` to unwrap backend response wrappers and ensure task payloads include `id`/`__ID`, then wired the task and timer endpoints to use those helpers. This addresses the missing API-layer normalization and keeps task/timer consumers from receiving `undefined` when the backend returns arrays or wrapped payloads.

Tests run:
- `npm test -- src/__tests__/tasksApi.test.js` (failed: `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'get/post')` because tests mock `axios` while `src/api/tasks.js` uses `dataService`).

Next steps:
1. Update `src/__tests__/tasksApi.test.js` to mock `dataService` (or its client) instead of `axios`.
2. Re-run `npm test -- src/__tests__/tasksApi.test.js`.
